assertion_results.text: Impact map or task descriptions explicitly flag at least 3 ambiguities from the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md contains a section 'Ambiguities Identified in Feature TC-9002' with 6 explicit ambiguities: (1) 'Search should be faster' — no target latency specified, (2) 'Results should be more relevant' — no relevance criteria defined, (3) 'Add filters' — 'some kind of filtering capability' is unspecified, (4) 'Better UI' — marked non-MVP, (5) 'Should be fast enough' — no performance threshold, (6) 'Don't break existing functionality' — vague NFR. This exceeds the 3-ambiguity minimum.

assertion_results.text: Plan acknowledges that 'Better UI' (non-MVP) cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository and excludes it from scope
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md mentions 'Better UI' in the ambiguities table with resolution 'Excluded from this plan per the feature's own MVP classification. Backend-only changes.' However, it does not explicitly state that Better UI cannot be planned without design mockups or a frontend repository. It only acknowledges it's non-MVP and excluded — the reasoning about missing design mockups or frontend repo is absent.

assertion_results.text: Tasks still follow the task-description-template.md format despite ambiguous requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks (task-1 through task-4) follow the template format with sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify (and Files to Create where applicable), Implementation Notes, Reuse Candidates, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, Verification Commands, and Dependencies. Optional sections like API Changes are included only where relevant (tasks 2 and 3). This matches the task-description-template.md structure.

assertion_results.text: Tasks document assumptions where they fill in missing details, labeled as assumptions pending clarification
assertion_results.evidence: The tasks make concrete design decisions to fill in ambiguous requirements (e.g., task 1 assumes tsvector/GIN indexes, task 3 assumes filters for entity_type/date_range/severity), but none of the four task descriptions contain the word 'assumption' or any section/label marking these decisions as 'assumptions pending clarification'. The ambiguities are acknowledged in the impact map but the tasks themselves do not label their gap-filling decisions as assumptions.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks contain a '## Repository' section with exactly one repository name: task-1: 'trustify-backend', task-2: 'trustify-backend', task-3: 'trustify-backend', task-4: 'trustify-backend'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (with substantive content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), and '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified in task-1-add-search-indexes.md, task-2-optimize-search-service.md, task-3-add-search-filters.md, and task-4-search-integration-tests.md.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Most file paths reference real paths from repo-backend.md, but task-3 has 'Files to Create: modules/search/src/model/mod.rs' — this path does not exist in the repo-backend.md directory tree. The repo-backend.md shows modules/search/ containing only src/lib.rs, src/service/mod.rs, and src/endpoints/mod.rs. There is no model/ directory under modules/search/. Also, task-1 creates 'migration/src/m0002_search_indexes/mod.rs' — while this follows the pattern of m0001_initial/, the specific path m0002_search_indexes/ is invented (though this is a new file creation following an established pattern, which is reasonable). The modules/search/src/model/mod.rs path in task-3 is clearly an invented path not derivable from the manifest.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 references: 'migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs' for migration pattern, 'entity/src/sbom.rs', 'entity/src/advisory.rs', 'entity/src/package.rs' for column names. Task 2 references: 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs', 'common/src/model/paginated.rs', 'common/src/error.rs', specific patterns like 'to_tsquery', 'ts_rank', 'plainto_tsquery'. Task 3 references: 'common/src/db/query.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/list.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs'. Task 4 references: 'tests/api/search.rs', 'tests/api/sbom.rs', 'tests/api/advisory.rs', specific patterns like 'assert_eq!(resp.status(), StatusCode::OK)'. All tasks reference concrete file paths and code patterns from the repository.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks contain '## Target Branch' followed by 'main': task-1 line 9, task-2 line 9, task-3 line 9, task-4 line 9.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: There are no output files containing description digest comments. The outputs directory contains only impact-map.md and four task description files. No file contains the string 'sha256-md:', 'sha256-adf:', '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:', or any 64-character hex string. There is no evidence that digest comments were posted.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: None of the four task descriptions contain any convention references in the prescribed format. There are no 'Per CONVENTIONS.md §...' references, no 'Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>' rationale strings in any task. The repo-backend.md mentions a CONVENTIONS.md file exists in the repository, but the tasks do not reference any conventions from it. Convention-aware enrichment appears to not have been performed at all.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'priority' with the inherited priority name. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the priority key is omitted entirely from additional_fields (not set to null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: The feature fixture (feature-ambiguous.md) shows Priority: Normal (not 'Undefined'), so every task should inherit the priority. However, none of the four task description files contain 'additional_fields', 'priority', or 'Normal' anywhere in their content. There is no evidence that priority was inherited or propagated to the tasks.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'fixVersions' with the inherited version(s). When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the fixVersions key is omitted entirely from additional_fields
assertion_results.evidence: The feature fixture shows 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.6.0' (non-empty). CLAUDE.md has no '### Jira Field Defaults' section, so fixVersion scope defaults to 'both', meaning tasks should inherit fixVersions. However, none of the four task description files contain 'additional_fields', 'fixVersions', or 'RHTPA 1.6.0'. There is no evidence that fixVersions were inherited or propagated to the tasks.

assertion_results.text: The summary comment on the feature issue (Step 6c) includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values that were propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: There is no output file representing a summary comment on the feature issue. The outputs directory contains only impact-map.md and four task files. No file mentions 'summary comment', 'Step 6c', inherited priority 'Normal', or inherited fixVersion 'RHTPA 1.6.0' in the context of a feature-level summary. There is no evidence that a summary comment was posted.
